response: The head coach of the Georgia Tech football team that finished the regular season with the same record as the 1981 team was George O'Leary. O'Leary served as the interim head coach for Georgia Tech's football team in 2001 after George O'Leary resigned and only coached for one game, the 2001 Seattle Bowl against No. 11-ranked Stanford. However, the question refers to a team that finished with the same record as the 1981 team, which was 1–10. This team was the 1994 Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets football team, which was led by head coach Bill Lewis and was fired after going 1-7. Defensive coordinator George O'Leary replaced Lewis as interim head coach for the remaining three games of the season.
